slices.name: Datasource Proxy Path Traversal
blind_spots: PathUnescape after route matching creates parser differential
AllowUnauthorizedAccess flag bypasses route auth
X-DS-Authorization header forwarded as Authorization

slices.name: Image Renderer File Write + ServeFile
blind_spots: No filepath.Rel confinement before ServeFile
Renderer auth token defaults to -
getNewFilePath safe but no defense-in-depth assertion

slices.name: Public Dashboard Annotation Timerange Bypass
slices.source: c.QueryInt64(from) + c.QueryInt64(to) from anonymous HTTP request
path: xorm_store.Get -> SQL WHERE: (if From > 0 && To > 0) omitted
blind_spots: from=0 and to=0 default values bypass timerange filter entirely
Tag-based annotations are org-wide

slices.name: Plugin Asset Path Traversal
blind_spots: CodeQL go/path-injection found at static.go:143,177
validatePath opt-in not applied globally

slices.name: Datasource TOCTOU Deletion
path: Inside txn: getDataSource [no SELECT FOR UPDATE]
DELETE FROM data_source WHERE org_id=? AND id=?
slices.sink: SQL DELETE (datasource deleted without TOCTOU protection)
blind_spots: ReadOnly check before transaction
No SELECT FOR UPDATE in re-fetch
5s TTL cache not invalidated on delete

slices.name: RBAC Scope Injection via URL Params
slices.source: web.Params(c.Req) URL params bound to scope template
path: HTTP request with user-controlled URL params
blind_spots: No validation that URL param matches expected format
Cache serves stale permissions for TTL duration
405 EvalPermission calls without scope parameter (Semgrep finding)

slices.name: Plugin Zip Extraction Symlink Traversal
path: Extract zip file entries -> isSymlink check
extractSymlink: read symlink target from archive
blind_spots: CodeQL go/unsafe-unzip-symlink: unresolved path from archive header used in symlink creation
isSymlinkRelativeTo check may not cover all traversal variants

slices.name: CSRF Bypass via User-Controlled Header
slices.source: HTTP Origin header + custom CSRF header (both user-controlled)
path: HTTP request with Origin header + custom csrf_additional_headers value
CSRF check: for h in c.headers -> customHost = r.Header.Get(h)
blind_spots: CodeQL user-controlled-bypass: both Origin and custom header are user-controlled
An attacker controlling both headers could set addr.Host == origin to pass CSRF check
slices.note: Only exploitable if csrf_additional_headers is configured (non-default). Requires ability to set both headers.

slices.name: SQL Expression INTO OUTFILE File Write
path: AllowQuery(name, query): walks AST, *sqlparser.Into ALLOWED at parser_allow.go:113
go-mysql-server executes INTO OUTFILE -> writes to filesystem
slices.sink: Filesystem write via go-mysql-server INTO OUTFILE
blind_spots: *sqlparser.Into on allowlist (parser_allow.go:113)
IsReadOnly bypassed via INTO.IsReadOnly()->SELECT.IsReadOnly()->true
WithDisableFileWrites(true) NOT called on mysql context (db.go:71)
secure_file_priv commented out (db.go:76-77)

slices.name: Avatar Anonymous Auth Bypass DoS
path: reqSignedIn middleware: AllowAnonymous=true when anon enabled
avatar.Handler: cache lookup miss for unique hash
Goroutine per unique hash; LRU max 2000 entries
slices.sink: Outbound HTTP to Gravatar (goroutine exhaustion DoS)
blind_spots: reqSignedIn allows anonymous; should be reqSignedInNoAnonymous
LRU bound mitigates but does not prevent burst DoS

slices.name: K8s Snapshot Delete Cross-Org via deleteKey
path: DB: DELETE FROM dashboard_snapshot WHERE delete_key=? [any org]
blind_spots: OrgID check present in REST API (api.go) but MISSING in K8s API path
deleteKey is 190-bit entropy but can leak via API responses/logs

slices.name: Cloud Migration CancelSnapshot Cross-Org
path: SQL: UPDATE cloud_migration_snapshot SET status=? WHERE session_uid=? AND uid=? [NO org_id]
blind_spots: CancelSnapshot interface has no orgID parameter
SQL WHERE clause missing org_id constraint
session_uid is UUID (globally unique but org-agnostic)

note: SLICE-009 through SLICE-012 added for audit 2026-03-21 based on KB high-priority targets.
